# cli

```go
import "im/internal/cli"
```

Package cli defines the im command\-line interface and input mode detection.

## Index

- [Variables](<#variables>)
- [type InputMode](<#InputMode>)
  - [func DetectInputMode\(args \[\]string\) InputMode](<#DetectInputMode>)
  - [func \(m InputMode\) String\(\) string](<#InputMode.String>)


## Variables

<a name="IsTerminal"></a>IsTerminal reports whether f is connected to a terminal. Exported as a variable for testability.

```go
var IsTerminal = func(f *os.File) bool {
    return term.IsTerminal(int(f.Fd()))
}
```

<a name="InputMode"></a>
## type InputMode

InputMode describes how the user is providing entry text.

```go
type InputMode int
```

<a name="ModeInline"></a>InputMode values.

```go
const (
    ModeInline InputMode = iota // positional args or after --
    ModePipe                    // stdin is piped
    ModeEditor                  // interactive editor session
)
```

<a name="DetectInputMode"></a>
### func DetectInputMode

```go
func DetectInputMode(args []string) InputMode
```

DetectInputMode determines how entry text will be sourced. Priority: explicit args \> piped stdin \> editor.

<a name="InputMode.String"></a>
### func \(InputMode\) String

```go
func (m InputMode) String() string
```

String returns a human\-readable label for the mode.
